---
title: 'Armor of God'
date: '2014-01-17T15:15:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2034812292257904399'
blogger_filename: '/2014/01/armor-of-god.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Daily Reading'
---

<h4>COME:</h4><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="http://holmechristianfellowship.org/romansword.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: right; float: right; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-left: 1em;"><img border="0" src="http://holmechristianfellowship.org/romansword.jpg" height="320" width="298" /></a></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><a href="http://gatewaydevotions.com/it-is-written/day-17" target="_blank">http://gatewaydevotions.com/it-is-written/day-17</a><em style="border: 0px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil. For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this age, against spiritual hosts of wickedness in the heavenly places.</em><cite style="border: 0px; color: #77533b; display: block; font-family: calluna, 'Trebuchet MS', Tahoma, Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-style: normal; letter-spacing: 0.5039999485015869px; line-height: 26.875px; margin: 0px 0px 20px; padding: 0px; text-align: center; text-transform: uppercase; width: 2062.6875px;"><a class="lbsBibleRef" data-reference="Ephesians 6.11–12" data-version="nkjv" href="http://biblia.com/bible/nkjv/Ephesians%206.11%E2%80%9312" style="border: 0px; color: #996b4c; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">EPHESIANS 6:11–12</a></cite><span style="color: #030404; font-family: calluna, 'Trebuchet MS', Tahoma, Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 17px; letter-spacing: 0.5039999485015869px; line-height: 26.875px;">- See more at: http://gatewaydevotions.com/it-is-written/day-17#sthash.UVwCoELa.dpuf</span></blockquote><br /><h4>HEAR:</h4>I need to spend more time in the word. And put that before the other things. Even in my new dovotional schedule it seem to be the one thing that get's pressed out for time.<br /><br /><br /><h4>DO:</h4>Put the WORD first, then prayer, then journal, then the quite time. Maybe I could even incorporate my reading for the day as a section of my journal and save posts on this site for longer studies... something to ponder.<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />
